blurb: System security encompasses the protections Apple applies from the initial power-on boot sequence through ongoing runtime integrity enforcement. The layered approach includes a hardware-rooted boot chain (Boot ROM → LLB → iBoot → kernel), cryptographic verification of every software component before execution, runtime kernel integrity checks, and process isolation via sandboxing and entitlement-based access control. On macOS, additional layers include System Integrity Protection, Gatekeeper, and XProtect malware detection, ensuring only Apple-authorized and user-approved software executes and that modifications to system software are immediately detectable.
